task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the calendar view for Week 3 (two weeks from next Monday).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calendar view displays the target week, showing the 3 original 'Security Workshop' events scheduled on Monday.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the first 'Security Workshop' event on Tuesday.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new event block appears on Tuesday at 09:00-10:30. The event creation form or calendar block shows the title 'Security Workshop: Phishing' (or matching the first Monday event) and location 'Training Room B'.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the second 'Security Workshop' event on Tuesday.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new event block appears on Tuesday at 11:00-12:30. The event creation form or calendar block shows the title 'Security Workshop: Data Protection' (or matching the second Monday event) and location 'Training Room B'.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the third 'Security Workshop' event on Tuesday.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new event block appears on Tuesday at 13:30-15:00. The event creation form or calendar block shows the title 'Security Workshop: Incident Response' (or matching the third Monday event) and location 'Training Room B'.

task_completion.description: The new Tuesday events have identical descriptions to the Monday originals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the event details or edit forms show that the description text for the Tuesday events exactly matches the text from the corresponding Monday events.

task_completion.description: The calendar shows exactly 6 events and no recurrence feature was used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final calendar week view clearly shows 3 events on Monday and 3 events on Tuesday (6 total). None of the Tuesday events display a recurrence/repeat icon (such as circular arrows), indicating they were created independently.

integrity.description: Agent used the calendar application GUI to create the events.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking on calendar slots, filling out event creation forms, and saving them within the web application interface.

integrity.description: Events are genuinely created in the calendar system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The events appear as proper blocks within the calendar grid view, not just as text typed into a notepad, search bar, or terminal.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open showing curl commands, python scripts, or database queries used to inject the events directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target week is Week 3 (two weeks from next Monday). The 3 original events on Monday are 'Security Workshop: Phishing' (09:00-10:30), 'Security Workshop: Data Protection' (11:00-12:30), and 'Security Workshop: Incident Response' (13:30-15:00). The agent must create 3 new events on Tuesday with identical titles, times, descriptions, and location ('Training Room B'). The final expected count is 6 'Security Workshop' events in that week. The recurrence feature must not be used.
